owasp_top10: A08:2021 - Software and Data Integrity Failures
cwe: CWE-444 - Inconsistent Interpretation of HTTP Requests (HTTP Request/Response Smuggling)
CWE-639 - Authorization Bypass Through User-Controlled Key
CWE-525 - Use of Web Browser Cache Containing Sensitive Information

threat.title: HTTP Cache Poisoning via Unkeyed Header Manipulation in CDN/Reverse Proxy
threat.description: HTTP cache poisoning occurs when attackers manipulate unkeyed inputs (headers, parameters) to inject malicious content into CDN, Redis, or Varnish caches. The poisoned cache serves attacker-controlled responses to legitimate users, enabling XSS, credential theft, and DoS attacks. This vulnerability affects Cloudflare, CloudFront, Fastly, Akamai, and self-hosted caching layers.

versions_affected: Cloudflare (all versions prior to 2020 cache key normalization)

attack.scenario:
1. Attacker identifies cacheable endpoint (e.g., /api/profile, /static/widget.js) with weak cache key normalization
2. Sends crafted request with unkeyed header (X-Forwarded-Host: evil.com) to poison cache
3. CDN/cache stores response with attacker-controlled content (XSS payload, redirect URL)
4. Legitimate users request the same resource and receive poisoned cached response
5. Attacker's XSS executes in victim browsers, stealing session tokens
6. Poisoned cache persists until TTL expires (hours/days), affecting thousands of users
prerequisites: Application uses CDN, Varnish, Redis, or other HTTP cache
Cache key does not include all relevant request components (Host, X-Forwarded-Host, etc.)
Cache hit rate is high (300+ seconds TTL) for targeted resources
No cache key normalization or validation of unkeyed inputs
Application reflects unkeyed headers in responses (e.g., X-Forwarded-Host in redirect URLs)

cve_references.description: HTTP/2 push denial of service via cache poisoning in libcurl, affecting CDN implementations
VMware vRealize Operations cache poisoning via Host header manipulation leading to SSRF
Cloudflare Workers cache poisoning via inconsistent header parsing between cache and origin

real_world_breaches.impact: Cache poisoning via path confusion allowed attackers to steal user data including transaction history and account balances
real_world_breaches.root_cause: Cloudflare cache served sensitive API responses due to path normalization mismatch between cache and origin

real_world_breaches.impact: Attackers poisoned CDN cache serving Firefox updates, potentially delivering malicious updates to millions
real_world_breaches.root_cause: X-Forwarded-Host header not included in cache key, allowing Host header override

bug_bounty_reports.summary: Cloudflare cache poisoning via X-Forwarded-Scheme header leading to XSS on multiple domains
Akamai CDN cache poisoning via Vary header bypass, serving cached admin responses to regular users

security_research.title: Practical Web Cache Poisoning
security_research.key_finding: 88% of tested websites vulnerable to cache poisoning via unkeyed headers (X-Forwarded-Host, X-Original-URL)

security_research.title: Web Cache Deception Attack
security_research.key_finding: Path confusion between cache and origin allows caching of sensitive authenticated responses

security_research.title: HTTP Desync Attacks: Request Smuggling Reborn
security_research.key_finding: Cache poisoning via HTTP request smuggling affects 40% of CDN providers including Cloudflare and CloudFront

mitigations.title: Implement Cache Key Normalization with Cloudflare Custom Cache Keys
mitigations.description: Configure CDN cache keys to include all relevant request components (Host, headers, query parameters) that affect response content. Use Cloudflare's Custom Cache Key API or CloudFront Cache Policies to explicitly define cache key composition.
iso27001_controls: A.14.1.2 - Securing application services on public networks
nist_csf: PR.DS-5 - Protections against data leaks are implemented
validation_steps: Test cache behavior with X-Forwarded-Host header variations
Verify cache key includes Host, Origin, and Referer headers
Use Param Miner (Burp extension) to identify unkeyed inputs
Confirm cache isolation between different host headers
Monitor cache hit ratio to ensure normalization doesn't break caching

mitigations.title: Validate and Strip Dangerous Headers with Vary Header Configuration
mitigations.description: Use Vary header to explicitly declare which headers affect response content. Strip or normalize dangerous headers (X-Forwarded-Host, X-Original-URL) at CDN edge before reaching origin.
validation_steps: Send request with X-Forwarded-Host: evil.com and verify it's blocked
Check response headers include proper Vary configuration
Test that authenticated responses are not cached
Verify cache respects Vary header across different header combinations
Use curl to test: curl -H 'X-Forwarded-Host: evil.com' https://example.com/api/profile

mitigations.title: Deploy Cache Poisoning Detection with SIEM and WAF Rules
mitigations.description: Implement real-time detection of cache poisoning attempts using WAF rules (Cloudflare, AWS WAF) and SIEM correlation to identify suspicious header manipulation patterns.

code_example.code:
// ============================================
// Cloudflare WAF Custom Rule (Expression)
// Dashboard: Security > WAF > Custom Rules
// ============================================

/*
Rule Name: Block Cache Poisoning Attempts
Expression:
*/

(
  // Detect X-Forwarded-Host with external domain
  (http.request.headers["x-forwarded-host"][0] ne http.host and
   not http.request.headers["x-forwarded-host"][0] in {"example.com" "www.example.com" "api.example.com"}) or
  
  // Detect X-Original-URL header (often used in cache poisoning)
  http.request.headers.names[*] eq "x-original-url" or
  
  // Detect unusual cache busting patterns
  (http.request.uri.path contains ".." or
   http.request.uri.path contains "//" or
   http.request.uri.path contains "%2e" or
   http.request.uri.path contains "%252e") or
  
  // Detect Host header with IP address (sign of cache poisoning test)
  http.host matches "^[0-9]{1,3}\\.[0-9]{1,3}\\.[0-9]{1,3}\\.[0-9]{1,3}$"
)

/*
Action: Block
Log: Enabled
Description: Blocks cache poisoning attempts via header manipulation
*/

// ============================================
// Splunk SIEM Detection Query
// ============================================

/*
Alert: Cache Poisoning Detection
Query:
*/

index=cdn_logs sourcetype=cloudflare:access
| eval has_forwarded_host=if(isnotnull('x_forwarded_host'), 1, 0)
| eval host_mismatch=if('x_forwarded_host'!='host' AND isnotnull('x_forwarded_host'), 1, 0)
| eval has_original_url=if(isnotnull('x_original_url'), 1, 0)
| eval suspicious_path=if(match(uri_path, "(\\.\\.|//|%2e|%252e)"), 1, 0)
| eval cache_poisoning_score=(has_forwarded_host * 2) + (host_mismatch * 5) + (has_original_url * 8) + (suspicious_path * 3)
| where cache_poisoning_score >= 5
| stats count as attempts, 
        values(x_forwarded_host) as forwarded_hosts,
        values(uri_path) as paths,
        values(src_ip) as source_ips,
        max(cache_poisoning_score) as max_score
  by host, _time
| where attempts > 10
| sort -max_score

// ============================================
// Azure Sentinel KQL Detection
// ============================================

/*
Rule Name: HTTP Cache Poisoning via Header Manipulation
Severity: High
Query:
*/

CDNAccessLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(1h)
| extend ForwardedHost = tostring(parse_json(RequestHeaders)["x-forwarded-host"])
| extend OriginalURL = tostring(parse_json(RequestHeaders)["x-original-url"])
| extend HostHeader = tostring(parse_json(RequestHeaders)["host"])
| where isnotnull(ForwardedHost) and ForwardedHost != HostHeader
    or isnotnull(OriginalURL)
    or RequestPath contains ".." or RequestPath contains "//"
| summarize 
    AttemptCount = count(),
    UniquePaths = dcount(RequestPath),
    SuspiciousHeaders = make_set(ForwardedHost),
    FirstSeen = min(TimeGenerated),
    LastSeen = max(TimeGenerated)
  by ClientIP, HostHeader, bin(TimeGenerated, 5m)
| where AttemptCount > 5
| project 
    TimeGenerated,
    ClientIP,
    HostHeader,
    AttemptCount,
    UniquePaths,
    SuspiciousHeaders,
    FirstSeen,
    LastSeen
| order by AttemptCount desc

// ============================================
// Elastic Stack Detection Rule (JSON)
// ============================================

{
  "name": "HTTP Cache Poisoning Detection",
  "description": "Detects cache poisoning attempts via unkeyed header manipulation",
  "risk_score": 73,
  "severity": "high",
  "type": "query",
  "query": "event.category:web AND (http.request.headers.x-forwarded-host:* OR http.request.headers.x-original-url:* OR url.path:(*..*)) AND NOT http.request.headers.x-forwarded-host:(example.com OR www.example.com)",
  "filters": [],
  "language": "kuery",
  "interval": "5m",
  "from": "now-6m",
  "to": "now",
  "actions": [
    {
      "group": "default",
      "id": "webhook-security-team",
      "params": {
        "message": "Cache poisoning detected from {{context.source.ip}}"
      }
    }
  ]
}

validation_steps: Create Cloudflare WAF rule and test with malicious headers
Configure SIEM to ingest CDN access logs
Test detection by sending: curl -H 'X-Forwarded-Host: evil.com' https://example.com
Verify alerts trigger in SIEM when threshold exceeded
Set up automated response to block repeat offenders

indicators: Sudden spike in cache misses after period of stable hits
Unusual X-Forwarded-Host, X-Original-URL headers in logs
User reports receiving content from wrong domain or language
Cache serving authenticated content to unauthenticated users
Abnormal Vary header values in cached responses
Path traversal patterns (../, //) in cached resource URLs
Multiple requests from same IP with varying Host headers
Cached responses containing JavaScript redirects to external domains

log_queries.query:
// Detect cache poisoning by correlating cache hits with suspicious headers
CDNAccessLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(1h)
| extend CacheStatus = tostring(parse_json(ResponseHeaders)["x-cache-status"])
| extend ForwardedHost = tostring(parse_json(RequestHeaders)["x-forwarded-host"])
| where CacheStatus in ("HIT", "STALE")
| where isnotnull(ForwardedHost) and ForwardedHost != Host
| summarize 
    PoisonedCacheHits = count(),
    UniqueVictimIPs = dcount(ClientIP),
    AttackerIP = any(ClientIP)
  by RequestPath, ForwardedHost, bin(TimeGenerated, 5m)
| where PoisonedCacheHits > 100 or UniqueVictimIPs > 10
| order by UniqueVictimIPs desc

index=cdn_logs sourcetype=cloudflare:access
| eval cache_status=if(match(response_headers, "X-Cache-Status: HIT"), "HIT", "MISS")
| eval forwarded_host=mvindex(split(mvfilter(match(request_headers, "X-Forwarded-Host:.*")), ": "), 1)
| where cache_status="HIT" AND isnotnull(forwarded_host) AND forwarded_host!=host
| stats count as poisoned_hits, dc(src_ip) as victim_count, earliest(_time) as first_seen, latest(_time) as last_seen by uri_path, forwarded_host
| where victim_count > 10
| eval impact_score=victim_count * poisoned_hits
| sort -impact_score
http.response.headers.x-cache-status: "HIT" AND http.request.headers.x-forwarded-host:* AND NOT http.request.headers.x-forwarded-host:(example.com OR www.example.com OR api.example.com)

tools.name: Param Miner (Burp Suite Extension)
tools.usage: Automatically identifies unkeyed inputs by fuzzing headers and parameters

tools.name: Cloudflare Cache Analytics
tools.usage: Dashboard > Analytics > Caching > Filter by cache status and origin errors
